title: Kolanjiappar Temple
tagline: Kolanjiappar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Kolanjiappar temple in Manavalanallur, a village on the outskirts of Virudhachalam in Cuddalore district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Muru
overview.description: Kolanjiappar temple in Manavalanallur, a village on the outskirts of Virudhachalam in Cuddalore district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Murugan. Constructed in the Dravidian architecture, the temple is located in the Virudhachalam - Manavalanallur Road. The legend of the temple is associated with Saivite saint Sundarar and the Vriddhagiriswarar temple ad Virudhachalam. The temple has a five-tiered gateway tower, the gopuram, leading to a pillared hall and the sanctum.

topPlaces.name: Vriddhachalam block
topPlaces.description: Vriddhachalam block is a revenue block of Cuddalore district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This revenue block consist of 51 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Vriddachalam division
topPlaces.description: Vriddachalam division is a revenue division in the Cuddalore district of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Vriddachalam taluk
topPlaces.description: Vriddachalam taluk is a taluk of Cuddalore district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Virudhachalam.

topPlaces.name: Virudhachalam
topPlaces.description: Virudhachalam, also called Vriddhachalam, is a Municipality and taluk headquarters in Cuddalore district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The ancient name of this town is Thirumudhukundram.

topPlaces.name: Veerateeswarar Temple, Thiruvathigai
topPlaces.description: Thiruvathigai Veerateswarar Temple (also Virattaneshwara Temple, Tamil: வீரட்டானேசுவரர் ) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva. It is situated in Thiruvathigai village which is about 2 kilometres east from the town of Panruti in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Kachirayanatham
topPlaces.description: Kachirayanatham is a village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village was originally ruled by a king called 'Kachirayan' leading to the naming of the village as Kachirayanatham.

Thiruvathigai Veerateswarar Temple (also Virattaneshwara Temple, Tamil: வீரட்டானேசுவரர் ) is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva. It is situated in Thiruvathigai village which is about 2 kilometres east from the town of Panruti in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, India.
Kachirayanatham is a village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village was originally ruled by a king called 'Kachirayan' leading to the naming of the village as Kachirayanatham.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kolanjiappar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kolanjiappar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kolanjiappar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (106 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (105 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kolanjiappar Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kolanjiappar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kolanjiappar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kolanjiappar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kolanjiappar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Vriddhachalam block, Vriddachalam division, Vriddachalam taluk, Virudhachalam, Veerateeswarar Temple, Thiruvathigai.
